title: Near-Orthogonal Latin Squares: Maximizing Satisfied Orthogonality
strategies: cyclic construction L_m[i][j]=(a_m*i+j+s_m) mod n (reproduces the checker baseline -> ~0.1)
intercalate (2x2 trade) hill-climbing from the cyclic start, single restart
multi-restart intercalate local search with randomized diversification of the starting squares -> markedly higher distinct-pair counts across the family
structured pair constructions (prolongation, Kronecker/product designs, group-based transversals) that push toward the open MOLS bounds
